overview: Accelerate your mana ahead of the curve, then deploy powerful expensive cards before the opponent can answer them. Survive the early turns, hit your mana milestones, and overwhelm the board with payoffs the opponent's deck is not built to handle.
win_conditions: Cast a game-ending threat or payoff well ahead of schedule
Bury the opponent under permanents they cannot match on mana

keep_criteria: Lands plus mana acceleration to power out your payoffs early
A curve that ramps on turns one to three into a real payoff
Enough early interaction or blockers to survive to your big turns
mulligan_criteria: All payoffs and no acceleration — you are simply too slow
All ramp and no payoff — you accelerate into nothing
Land-light hands that cannot get the ramp engine started

examples.reason: Lands, a turn-two ramp spell, and a strong payoff is a clean curve-out.
Four expensive payoffs with no acceleration will die before they come online.

early_game: Prioritize mana acceleration — every ramp spell moves your payoff up a turn
Use cheap interaction or blockers only to survive, not to develop a board
Sequence lands and ramp to hit your key mana number on time
mid_game: Deploy your first big payoff and force the opponent to answer it
Keep a second threat in reserve so a single removal spell does not reset you
Stabilize the board with payoffs that also defend
late_game: Your mana advantage should be decisive — out-resource and overwhelm
Close quickly once ahead; do not give an aggressive opponent extra draws

key_cards.name: Mana acceleration
key_cards.notes: Ramp spells and mana creatures that move your payoffs ahead of the curve.

key_cards.name: Payoffs
key_cards.notes: Expensive, high-impact cards the opponent is not prepared to answer early.

key_cards.name: Early interaction
key_cards.notes: Cheap removal or blockers to reach the turn your payoffs take over.

sequencing_tips: Always play ramp before payoffs — count the mana you will have next turn
Hold a backup threat against decks with efficient removal
Use removal to survive, not to develop; your payoffs are the real plan

matchups.advice: Prioritize survival: cheap blockers and removal, then stabilize with a payoff.
Ramp into threats faster than they can answer; bait removal before the key payoff.
watch_for: spot removal on your mana creatures

common_threats: Fast aggro that kills you before the ramp pays off
Removal aimed at your mana creatures that sets you back a full turn
Counterspells that answer your payoff after you have tapped out
